most_recent_game.recap_text:
KNOXVILLE, Tenn. –
Mizzou baseball picked up a series-opening victory, 8-4, over No. 22 Tennessee on Friday night at Lindsey Nelson Stadium. The win marked Mizzou's first SEC win of the season.
The Tigers (16-6, 1-3 SEC) received an offensive spark from OF
Pierre Seals
who went opposite field for his second home run of the year to give MU a 3-0 lead that they would not surrender. LHP
Javyn Pimental
went four innings in his sixth start of the season, letting up just one Tennessee (15-7, 1-3 SEC) run on a solo shot, and allowed four hits and two walks while recording a pair of strikeouts.
Each side generated baserunners in the early going as the only 1-2-3 frame was the home half of the second, but neither threat resulted in runs until Mizzou posted the first tally in the third. First baseman
Tyler Macon
led the inning off with a single out of the nine-hole and proceeded to steal second base.
Jase Woita
drove Macon home for the first run, then Seals hit a two-run blast to put the Tigers up, 3-0.
Mizzou left
Blaize Ward
stranded after a leadoff double in the fourth, then the Vols got on the board on a Stone Lawless solo home run.
The Tigers answered immediately in the top of the fifth, as
Kaden Peer
and
Kam Durnin
strung together back-to-back singles before
Mateo Serna
was hit by a pitch on an 0-2 count. With the bases loaded, Ward delivered a two-run single up the middle, making it 5-1 Mizzou as the entire rally came with two outs.
Righty
JD Dohrmann
relieved Pimental with a scoreless fifth inning, then UT was able to cut into Missouri's lead in the sixth as the first two batters of the inning reached and came around to score to make it a 5-3 ballgame.
The Tiger offense again had the answer for Tennessee's bats as a rally started by Serna's walk in an eight-pitch battle led to a Ward single, followed by the big blast of the inning – a  three-run shot by
Keegan Knutson
to give MU an 8-3 cushion. It was Knutson's first HR of the year and third in the Black & Gold.
The Volunteers led off the seventh with another solo homer, making it an 8-4 lead, but Dohrmann would retire the next three Vols in order.  He came back out for the eighth and worked another scoreless frame.
In the ninth, a pair of walks issued by Dohrmann forced the Tigers to go to the bullpen to freshman
Isaiah Salas
. Another walk led to another pitching change the very next at-bat as Mizzou turned to fellow freshman
Sam Rosand
, who responded with a strikeout to strand the bases loaded and clinch the MU victory, 8-4.
NOTEBOOK
Ward notched his first career three-hit game, going 3-for-4, and tied a career-best with two RBI.
Woita logged his ninth multi-hit game of the year, going 2-for-5 with a double and an RBI.
Seals notched his eighth multi-hit game this season and knocked his Missouri-best 17
th
career HR. It was also Seals' fifth multi-RBI game this season.
Peer had a pair of hits for the fourth time this season and extended his on-base streak to 10 games (every game he has played this season).
Knutson slugged his first homer of the campaign and drove in three runs for the second time this season.
Pimental went four innings, allowing a run on four hits and striking out a pair of Vols.
Dohrmann went four innings in relief, allowing five hits and three runs while fanning four batters. He earned the win to improve to 2-1 on the season.
Mizzou improved to 18-16 all time against Tennessee and picked up their ninth win in Knoxville.
With the win, the Tigers have matched last season's win total through just 22 games (16-6).
Rosand earned his second save of the season, striking out the only Vol he faced.
The Tigers are back at Lindsey Nelson Stadium tomorrow at 5 p.m. CT for a chance to clinch the series over the No. 22 Volunteers. The entire series can be streamed live on SEC Network+. All games can also be streamed on the
Varsity Network by Learfield
, with longtime Mizzou play-by-play announcer Tex Little on the call and Matt Michaels providing color.
FOLLOW THE TIGERS
For all the latest information on Mizzou baseball, please visit
MUTigers.com
. For up-to-the-minute updates, follow the Tigers on
Twitter
,
Instagram
and
Facebook
.
